name: born-a-crime
description: Trevor Noah's memoir of a South African childhood — surviving apartheid as a mixed-race child, using language as armor, defying the system's limits, and the unbreakable partnership with his rebel mother.
useCase: Navigating identity across cultures, code-switching for survival, breaking the cycle of abuse, escaping the trap of assigned limits, building an intergenerational partnership.
knownRisks: Contains graphic descriptions of domestic violence that may be triggering
The context of apartheid South Africa is specific and may not translate directly
Trevor's humor about painful subjects may not work for all readers
